user_message: Robert Singh at Blue Yonder Airlines just sent this. Help me triage this.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-12 08:46 EST
Subject: Re: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with pipeline hygiene. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-02-01 13:41 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Blue Yonder Airlines pipeline hygiene

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with pipeline hygiene. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-19 13:07 EST
Subject: Follow-up: pipeline hygiene Review

There's been a change in our timeline for pipeline hygiene. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF40-L trap (Requiring approval for an obvious milestone update derived from the email create); produces complete Commitment assessment, BPF advance verdict, milestone proposal, forecast category recommendation, ac
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF40-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF40-L failure mode (Requiring approval for an obvious milestone update derived from the email create)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF40-L -- Requiring approval for an obvious milestone update derived from the email create
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
